landing.heroBody: Stones (milestones), XP tasks, Kanban, Timeline and Panel. Invite by @username — email stays private. Gamified, multi-project, in the cloud.
landing.featStonesBody: Milestones with color, dates and nested tasks. Visual progress instantly.
landing.featGameBody: XP, levels and rewards when you close tasks. The roadmap feels alive.
landing.featTeamBody: Invite by @username. Owner / admin / member roles and transferable ownership.
landing.featMultiBody: Create boards, manage members, leave or delete with danger controls.
landing.waitBody: Leave your email. The platform admin invites in arrival order, in batches, when there is room. No spam: only the Supabase invite email when it is your turn.
landing.waitBullet2: · You choose @username and password at setup
landing.waitBullet3: · You can decline and we remove your Auth footprint
landing.timelineHint: Click the dots to complete milestones

login.title: Piedra a Piedra
login.subtitle: Invite-only access
login.footer: No public sign-up. If you were invited, use the email link to set username and password.

join.title: Create your account
join.invitedAs: You were invited to Piedra a Piedra
join.chooseHint: Pick a public username and a password. Your email is not shared with other users.
join.displayPlaceholder: How others see you on the board
join.usernameRules: Only a-z, 0-9 and _ · no spaces · not «admin»
join.declineHint: Don't want to join? You can decline and we will remove your email from Authentication and the invite list.
join.decline: I don't want to create an account
join.declinedBody: No account was created. Your email was removed from platform access.
join.linkBody: Open the invite email link (it may expire). If you already have an account, sign in with email and password.

username.help1: It is your public identity on the platform (for example @maria).
username.help2: Other members invite you to projects with your username, not your email.
username.help3: Your email stays private: the system only uses it for access and platform invites.
username.help4: You can change it anytime from your profile.

projects.empty: You have no projects yet. Create one or accept an invite.
projects.emptyCta: Create your first roadmap and start earning XP
projects.hubSubtitle: Pick a board and keep stacking stones

platformInvite.title: Invitations
platformInvite.adminUnlimited: Unlimited invites. Choose how many invites the new user will get.
platformInvite.userConsume: Uses 1 of your {{n}}. The invitee gets 3.
platformInvite.quotaHelp: Existing user. The number is absolute (not added).
platformInvite.waitOrder: Order: oldest first. Batch invites by sign-up date.
platformInvite.noQuota: You have no invite quota. Ask the platform admin.

views.noActiveStones: No stones active on this date.
views.showAllHint: Turn on «Show all» or check dates / periods
views.emptyProjectHint: Create the first roadmap stone to start adding tasks.

profile.title: Your username
profile.usernameRules: Lowercase letters, numbers and _ only · no spaces · not «admin» and similar

projectSettings.inviteHint: Only users already on the platform. Email is not shared.
projectSettings.transferHint: The new owner must already be a member. You become admin.
projectSettings.leaveOwnerHint: You are the owner: transfer ownership to another member before leaving.
projectSettings.leaveNoMembers: No other members. Invite someone and transfer, or delete the project.
projectSettings.leaveHint: You will stop seeing the project and its tasks.
projectSettings.deleteWarn: Deleting the project is irreversible: stones, tasks, invites and files are removed.
projectSettings.deleteMembersHint: There are {{n}} other member(s). If you only want to leave, use Leave project and transfer ownership instead of deleting.
projectSettings.typeName: Type the project name to confirm:
projectSettings.onlyOwnerAdminRename: Only owner or admin can rename the project.
projectSettings.transferConfirm: Transfer ownership to @{{user}}? You will become project admin.
projectSettings.ownerMustTransfer: As owner you must choose who receives ownership
projectSettings.leaveConfirmOwner: You will transfer ownership and leave the project. Continue?
projectSettings.typeExact: Type the exact project name to confirm

config.missingTitle: Supabase not configured in this build
config.missingBody: Set VITE_* on Vercel and Redeploy.

hub.title: Documentation
hub.lead: Guides for Piedra a Piedra: getting started, .stones format, workspace views and team. Pick a topic or browse the sidebar.
hub.helpBody: If you just joined, start with the onboarding guide. If you already have a text plan, go straight to the .stones format.

start.cardDesc: How the app works, invite-only access and your first project.
start.title: Getting started
start.lead: Piedra a Piedra is a gamified multi-project roadmap: stones (milestones), tasks with XP and three views of the same board.
start.whatP1: You organize work into stones (milestones). Each stone groups tasks. Completing tasks earns XP and levels you up. The same project appears as Kanban, Timeline or Panel.
start.whatLi1: milestones with color, icon, dates and description
start.whatLi2: checkboxes with XP, assignees, period and image
start.whatLi3: multiple boards; each with its own team and roles
start.whatLi4: text format to import and export roadmaps
start.accessP1: There is no public signup. You only enter if invited (platform admin or someone with invite quota).
start.access1: You get a Supabase Auth invite email.
start.access2: Open the link and pick @username + password (or decline and the Auth user is removed).
start.access3: Sign in at /login with email and password.
start.access4: You land on the projects hub.
start.accessNote: You can also join the waitlist on the landing; the admin invites in batches when there is room.
start.firstP: From the projects hub you can:
start.first1: Create an empty project (name, description, dates).
start.first2: Add stones and tasks from the workspace.
start.first3: Or import a .stones file and fill the board at once — see
start.first4: Invite team members when you want to collaborate.
start.flowP: Inside a project switch views with the top-bar selector:
start.flowKanbanD: columns per stone; drag cards and mark done.
start.flowTimelineD: time line of milestones and progress.
start.flowPanelD: control / summary view of the project.
start.flowXp: Your level and XP for the project show in the top bar. Completing tasks adds XP.
start.nextP: Go deeper into the text format, views or team system:

stones.cardDesc: Intuitive text markup to create and share stones and tasks.
stones.title: .stones format
stones.lead: Write your roadmap in natural-language text, import it into Piedra a Piedra, and edit it in Kanban, Timeline or Panel. You can also export any project to .stones anytime.
stones.introP1: A .stones file is plain text with a simple, intuitive markup. You describe the project, stones (milestones) and tasks like a notepad plan — no JSON or YAML required.
stones.introP2: The app understands it on import and writes the same format on export. Great for templates, backups and sharing roadmaps by email or git.
stones.introLi3: Images are referenced by filename (upload them in the app after import)
stones.anatomyP: A typical file has four layers, in this order:
stones.anatomy3d: each milestone with banner, attributes and description
stones.headerP: First line is the project title; optionally a subtitle on the next line:
stones.metaP: Right after the header you can open a block of key: value pairs. Each line is a project property.
stones.metaOther: Any other key is kept in meta and re-exported (e.g. author, theme).
stones.stoneP: Each stone is framed with lines of dashes or double lines (═). Between them goes PIEDRA N · Name and optional attributes. Then free description paragraphs until ### Tareas.
stones.stoneAlt: Markdown style without a banner also works:
stones.fDates: optional ISO dates for the stone
stones.fId: optional id (otherwise generated from the title)
stones.stoneSep: Between stones you can use a short --- separator. The number after PIEDRA sets order (1, 2, 3…).
stones.tasksP: Under ### Tareas, each item is a Markdown-style checkbox. Task metadata is indented (spaces or tab) or key: value on the following lines.
stones.tImg: image filename (no folder; upload later in the app)
stones.tAssignees: platform usernames comma-separated (resolved if those members exist; usually ignored on fresh import)
stones.tasksIndent: Important: task fields must be more indented than the - [ ] item, or be key: value lines right under the task title.
stones.cheatTasks: Task list for the current stone
stones.exampleP: Copy, save as my-plan.stones and import from the projects hub:
stones.ieImportD: Projects → «Import .stones». Creates a new project with every stone and task from the file.
stones.ieExportD: Inside a project → Settings → «Export .stones». Downloads the current roadmap in this format.
stones.ieNote: Round-trip: export then import again keeps structure (image names and assignees depend on the target project).
stones.limit1: Images are not embedded: filename only (img: photo.png). After import, upload them in the app.
stones.limit2: assignees with @username re-export; on import into a new project without those members they are not assigned.
stones.limit3: Task done state is kept with - [x]. Stone order follows the PIEDRA N number.
stones.limit4: ES/EN synonyms (tiempo/time, notas/notes, icon/icono…) are supported by the parser.
stones.tip1: Use a simple text editor (VS Code, Notepad). Save as UTF-8.
stones.tip2: Default colors are assigned if you omit color on a stone.
stones.tip3: Keep task titles on one line; details go in notas: …
stones.tip4: No HTML or JSON needed: if it reads well in a notepad, the parser almost always gets it.
stones.tip5: Templates: export a “model” project and re-import it for each client or quarter.

workspace.cardDesc: Kanban, Timeline, Panel, tasks and XP inside a project.
workspace.title: Workspace & views
workspace.lead: Each project is a board with three views, a fixed top bar (view + level/XP) and tools for tasks and settings.
workspace.overviewP: Opening a project enters the workspace. The fixed top bar holds:
workspace.overviewLi2: Your level and XP in that project
workspace.kanbanP: Columns = stones. Cards = tasks. Best for day-to-day work and drag-and-drop.
workspace.kanbanLi1: Scroll horizontally on mobile if there are many stones.
workspace.kanbanLi2: Mark tasks done from the card or detail.
workspace.kanbanLi3: Create tasks from the column or the New Task flow.
workspace.timelineP: Shows the roadmap over time: milestones, progress and a chronological reading of the plan.
workspace.timelineLi1: Useful for presentations and seeing the project arc.
workspace.panelP: Control summary: project status at a glance.
workspace.panelLi2: Review progress without opening every card.
workspace.tasksP: The new/edit task modal covers the model fields:
workspace.tasksImageD: optional attachment; can be removed later
workspace.xpP: Gamification is per project: completing tasks adds XP and levels you up on that board.
workspace.xpLi1: Task XP is set on create or in .stones (xp: N).
workspace.xpLi2: Level and XP show in the workspace top bar.
workspace.xpLi3: Motivates the team without replacing real planning.
workspace.settingsP: From project settings (owner/admin depending on action) you can:
workspace.settingsLi1: Edit name, description and start/end dates.
workspace.settingsLi3: Manage members, roles and leave or delete the project (with confirmations).

team.cardDesc: Invite-only access, roles, @username and invite quotas.
team.title: Team & invites
team.lead: The platform is private: no open signup. Email is for Auth; in the app you are identified by @username.
team.inviteOnlyP: Nobody can self-register. Typical flow: waitlist → platform admin invite → signup with username and password.
team.inviteOnlyLi1: Landing: join the waitlist with email.
team.inviteOnlyLi2: Platform admin: invites in batches when there is room.
team.inviteOnlyLi3: Users with quota: can also invite others (default limit).
team.rolesP: Each project has its own team with roles:
team.roleOwner: owner; can transfer ownership, delete the project and manage everything.
team.roleAdmin: can manage members and most settings.
team.roleMember: collaborates on stones and tasks; no full admin powers.
team.inviteP: From the hub or the app invite flow:
team.invite1: Provide the person's email (Auth) — the UI keeps email private in day-to-day use.
team.invite2: They get the mail, set username + password (or decline).
team.invite3: After accepting they can join projects and be assigned to tasks.
team.inviteQuota: Platform invites have a quota (e.g. 3 by default). The admin can grant extra quotas to existing users.
team.usernameP: Username is your public identity in Piedra a Piedra.
team.usernameLi3: Email is not used as the board handle.
team.leaveP: When you leave or are removed from a project:
team.leaveLi1: You stop being a member and lose board access.
team.leaveLi2: Task assignees are purged so no ghost assignments remain.
team.leaveLi3: The owner must transfer ownership before leaving if they are the sole owner.

nim.title: NVIDIA NIM
nim.lead: Connect your free API key from build.nvidia.com to edit roadmaps with AI under review (diff + confirm).
nim.keyLocal: Stored only in this browser. Not persisted on our servers.

ai.promptPlaceholder: E.g. Add 3 tasks to @piedra1 about market validation. Higher xp for priorities…
ai.needKey: Set your NVIDIA NIM API key in profile settings
ai.safeNote: Nothing is saved until you confirm the diff.
ai.reviewLead: Review what matters: New, Changes (before/after .stones) and Removed. Include or exclude each block before saving.
ai.noChangesDetected: The model proposed no detectable changes
ai.fullFileDiffHint: Comparison of the text exported before the AI call and the model output.
